mnemonics.title: ENTROPY MEMORIZATION
mnemonics.content: Remember entropy increases with disorder: PURE DATA = 0 ENTROPY, MIXED DATA = HIGH ENTROPY. Think of ENTROPY as how SURPRISED you are about the class of a random instance.

mnemonics.title: DECISION TREE NODES
mnemonics.content: RIPE: Root Internal Parent Exit (leaf). Root starts, Internal tests, Parent leads to children, Exit (leaf) provides class label.

mnemonics.title: INFORMATION GAIN FORMULA
mnemonics.content: Information Gain = Entropy (parent) - Weighted Average Entropy (children). HIGHER GAIN = BETTER SPLIT = MORE PURE CHILDREN.

acronyms.usage: Decision tree algorithm using information gain for attribute selection
Decision tree algorithm using Gini index for binary classification
Decision tree algorithm using gain ratio to handle numerical attributes
Measure of impurity in decision trees, used by CART algorithm

visualTips.visualization: Imagine an actual tree upside down with roots at top. ROOT is the trunk starting point, INTERNAL NODES are branches splitting into smaller paths, and LEAF NODES are the end fruits or leaves providing the final decision.
Think of a jar with colored balls. PURE jar (one color) = 0 entropy = no surprise. MIXED jar (many colors) = high entropy = surprising to predict which color you'll pick.
Think of information gain as the CLARITY you gain after splitting. The more organized (pure) the groups become after splitting, the higher the information gain.
Draw a tree with too many branches and leaves like a dense bush. Compare with a pruned tree that looks like a well-maintained ornamental plant - simpler but more useful.

analogies.analogy: Like playing 20 questions to guess something. Each question (internal node) splits possibilities, and the final answer (leaf node) is your guess.
Like the CEO making the most important first decision that divides the entire company into major departments.
Entropy is like the messiness of your desk. A clean desk (one pile) has 0 entropy. A messy desk with papers everywhere has high entropy.

analogies.anal analogy: Pruning is like trimming a bonsai tree - cutting away unnecessary branches to make it more beautiful and healthier.
